entries.title: Future book reveals obey active Context

context.label: Golden Trio context-sensitive future reveal guard

content.fact: Later-book deaths, secret identities, Horcrux/Hallows knowledge, prophecy details, and final-war outcomes must not leak into earlier Contexts.
content.injection: Enforce active Context over model memory: keep later-book reveals out of narration and dialogue until the relevant year/anchor is active or accepted story lore explicitly changes the timeline.
constraints: This is a broad guard; narrower year-specific cards override it with exact timing.
Use it when a model tries to import later-book knowledge into an earlier scene.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Golden Trio context-sensitive future reveal guard; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not allow Hermione, Ron, Harry, teachers, or narration to casually reference future secrets before their discovery windows.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Horcruxes are not common wizarding knowledge

context.label: Before and during Horcrux discovery arc

content.fact: Horcruxes are obscure, taboo, and not ordinary wizarding vocabulary; even clever students should not know the mechanics without a specific discovery path.
content.injection: Treat Horcrux knowledge as restricted: before the Year 6 Slughorn-memory path, block casual use of the term or mechanics by Harry, Ron, Hermione, teachers, or ordinary wizards unless the story explicitly establishes early discovery.
constraints: Dumbledore and Voldemort may have private knowledge earlier; that does not make the knowledge public or available to students.
Hermione’s intelligence supports research after a clue; it does not grant unearned future knowledge.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before and during Horcrux discovery arc; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not treat Horcruxes as classroom material, library trivia, or common Auror vocabulary in earlier years.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Deathly Hallows quest is not active before Year 7

content.fact: Before the Year 7 Hallows explanation, the Hallows are not an active quest framework for the trio.
content.injection: Before the Hallows are introduced, do not have Harry, Ron, or Hermione reason in terms of the Elder Wand, Resurrection Stone, Cloak trio, Hallows symbol, or Hallows-versus-Horcrux choice unless accepted story lore introduced that path early.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Xenophilius explains the Hallows; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import Hallows vocabulary into Years 1-6 as if the trio is already pursuing it.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Snape loyalty remains hidden until memories

content.fact: Snape’s true long-term loyalty, love for Lily, and Dumbledore-arranged role remain hidden until Harry receives Snape’s memories near the end of Year 7.
content.injection: Before the Snape memories Context, write Snape’s motives as opaque or apparently hostile; do not let narration or characters confidently reveal his full loyalty, Lily motivation, or Dumbledore plan unless this story has already exposed it.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Snape memories reveal; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not let later fandom shorthand turn Snape into openly known Dumbledore-loyal ally before the memory reveal.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

content.fact: Before the end of Year 5, Harry does not know the full prophecy; most characters never know it unless directly told.
content.injection: Block full prophecy exposition before Dumbledore explains it after the Department of Mysteries; characters may fear a prophecy or seek it, but should not quote or reason from the complete wording without an established source.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Dumbledore explains the prophecy; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not make the prophecy common Order, Ministry, or student knowledge.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
